name: Saceruela from Almadén — circular via Valdemanco de Esteras
name_original: Saceruela desde Almadén — circular por Valdemanco de Esteras
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Almadén traverse a landscape characterized by well-preserved indigenous flora, including holm oaks and cork oaks, alongside pastures and Mediterranean scrubland. The region features significant geological heritage, notably the world's largest known mercury deposit. Cyclists can experience varied terrain, from pleasant forest scenery to more open areas offering panoramic views. Best touring cycling routes around Almadén The most popular touring cycling route is Almadén — circular route through Chillón, a 21.5 miles (34.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a balanced ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Saceruela from Almadén — circular via Valdemanco de Esteras, a difficult 50.4 miles (81.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, passing through varied landscapes with extensive views. Local touring cyclists also love the Chillón loop from Chillón, a 15.3 miles (24.7 km) trail leading through rural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Touring cycling around Almadén is defined by holm oak forests, Mediterranean scrubland, and open pastures. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 5 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almadén's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling around Almadén?
A: Touring cyclists in Almadén will encounter diverse landscapes characterized by well-preserved indigenous flora, including extensive holm oak and cork oak forests. You'll also ride through open pastures and classic Mediterranean scrubland. The region is known for its significant geological heritage, notably the world's largest known mercury deposit, and offers varied terrain from pleasant forest scenery to open areas with panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in Almadén?
A: Yes, Almadén offers several circular routes perfect for touring cyclists. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057586866/almaden-circular-route-through-chillon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén — circular route through Chillón&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 21.5-mile (34.7 km) trail. Another highly-rated circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057556196/saceruela-from-almaden-circular-via-valdemanco-de-esteras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saceruela from Almadén — circular via Valdemanco de Esteras&lt;/a&gt;, a more challenging 50.4-mile (81.1 km) path with significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Almadén?
A: The region's climate, particularly around Almadén (Ciudad Real), generally allows for year-round cycling. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures. While Pico Almadén in Jaén province can be accessible even in winter, some higher-altitude sections might experience snow, so checking local weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in the Almadén area?
A: For cyclists seeking a challenge, the Almadén region provides options with significant elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057556196/saceruela-from-almaden-circular-via-valdemanco-de-esteras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saceruela from Almadén — circular via Valdemanco de Esteras&lt;/a&gt; route is classified as difficult, covering 50.4 miles (81.1 km) with over 1000 meters of ascent. Additionally, the broader region includes Pico Almadén in Jaén, known as the highest road climb in the province, offering a demanding ascent with panoramic views, though parts of its road surface can be challenging for standard road bikes.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore along touring cycling routes in Almadén?
A: Almadén is rich in history, particularly its mining heritage. Along your rides, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Mining Park&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Museum&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable landmarks include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Bullring&lt;/a&gt; and the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Mary of the Star&lt;/a&gt;. The area around Almadén de la Plata also features the Neolithic site of Los Covachos and an ancient Roman road.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Almadén?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations depend on individual family capabilities, routes classified as 'moderate' are generally more accessible. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chillón loop from Chillón&lt;/a&gt;, at 15.3 miles (24.7 km) and taking about 2 hours, offers a pleasant ride through rural areas that could be suitable for families with some cycling experience. The general landscape of pastures and holm oak forests provides a scenic and relatively gentle environment for many sections.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for touring cycling routes in Almadén?
A: The touring cycling routes in Almadén vary in length and duration to suit different preferences. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057586866/almaden-circular-route-through-chillon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén — circular route through Chillón&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 21.5 miles (34.7 km) and typically takes around 3 hours. For a longer ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057556196/saceruela-from-almaden-circular-via-valdemanco-de-esteras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saceruela from Almadén — circular via Valdemanco de Esteras&lt;/a&gt; spans about 50.4 miles (81.1 km) and can take over 5 hours to complete, depending on your pace.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the touring cycling routes in Almadén?
A: The touring cycling routes in Almadén offer a variety of scenic views. You'll pass through charming forest scenery, particularly in areas with holm oaks and cork oaks. As you gain elevation, especially on routes like the challenging ascent towards Pico Almadén, you'll be rewarded with expansive panoramic views of the surrounding countryside, including most of the Jaén province. Expect vistas of traditional Mediterranean scrubland and open pastures.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there to explore around Almadén?
A: There are at least 3 highly-rated touring cycling routes available around Almadén, catering to different skill levels. These routes showcase the best of the region's diverse landscapes, from moderate loops through rural areas to more challenging long-distance paths with significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Almadén?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the well-preserved natural environments, the variety of landscapes from forests to open pastures, and the opportunity to combine cycling with exploring the rich historical sites, particularly the UNESCO World Heritage mercury mines.

Q: Are there any specific natural features or wildlife to look out for while cycling?
A: Yes, the Almadén region is known for its 'nature in its purest state.' You'll cycle through areas with well-preserved indigenous flora, including extensive holm oaks and cork oaks. The local fauna is abundant, with sightings of wild animals like wild boar and deer, coexisting with domestic livestock. The region's geological significance, as the site of the world's largest known mercury deposit, also makes it a unique natural heritage area.
tips:
Sculptural work that honors all the miners of Almadén and the region, chosen by popular subscription and made by Leovigildo Gallardo Blanco from Almaden.
The Monument to the Miner represents the history of more than two thousand years, the hard and sacrificial work of the miner and the history of a people; the rebellion against the exploitation of man for a miserable salary and the testimony of a region that jointly achieved the objectives it set itself. Opened in 1996.
Road between the towns of Valdemanco del Esteras and Sacaruela, asphalt in perfect condition, recently renovated, little traffic, a pleasure.
A 1 km gravel road that takes us from Saceruela to a small lagoon (Embalse de las Raíces).

waypoints.name: Quiet road between Valdemanco del Esteras and Sacaruela
Gravel road to a small lagoon
